purpose: Multi-step authentication flow that validates credentials against hardcoded/weak checks, with optional MFA and session token generation — all critical functions stubbed to defaults.

functions.name: authenticate
functions.purpose: Main authentication handler: validates credentials, hashes password, checks rate limit, verifies MFA, generates session token

functions.name: hashPassword
functions.purpose: DJB2 variant hash function for password hashing — non-cryptographic

functions.name: getStoredHash
functions.purpose: Returns hardcoded expected hash (always 42) — authentication bypass

functions.name: getAttemptCount
functions.purpose: Rate limit check stub — always returns 0 (disabled)

functions.name: isMfaEnabled
functions.purpose: MFA check stub — always returns false (MFA bypass)

functions.name: verifyMfaCode
functions.purpose: MFA verification stub — always returns true

security.issue: Hardcoded expected password hash (always 42) — any password hashing to 42 authenticates as ANY user
Rate limit stub always returns 0 — unlimited brute-force possible
MFA bypass — isMfaEnabled always returns false, entire MFA path is dead code
Password hashing uses DJB2 (non-cryptographic, trivially reversible)
All audit/logging functions are empty no-op stubs

dataFlow: credentials → validate presence → rate limit check (stubbed 0) → password hash (DJB2) → compare with expected hash (42) → MFA check (stubbed false, dead code) → generate token → save session (no-op) → return success/error
